markers.source_table: Inferred from cell type name - ferroportin, the sole known cellular iron exporter; Supplementary Table 3 (DEGs for refined prenatal cell states)
Main text - predicted ligand-receptor interaction CXCL8-ACKR1 between macrophages and endothelial cells (Extended Data Fig. 9a, Supplementary Table 28)
Main text - predicted ligand-receptor interaction CCL8-ACKR1 between macrophages and endothelial cells (Extended Data Fig. 9a, Supplementary Table 28)

other_findings.finding: Iron-recycling macrophages are one of four macrophage subsets identified in prenatal skin (LYVE1+, MHCII+, TML, iron-recycling)
other_findings.source_table: Main text, section 'Macrophages support prenatal skin angiogenesis'

other_findings.finding: Iron-recycling macrophages promote endothelial cell chemotaxis (gene module expression profiles)
All four macrophage subsets including iron-recycling expressed gene programs that drive angiogenesis
Gene set enrichment analysis performed on over-expressed genes in iron-recycling macrophages using Gene Ontology Biological Process 2023 and MSigDB Hallmark 2020 databases
Macrophage subsets annotated using marker genes from Suo et al. (2022)
Macrophages co-located with endothelial cells in neurovascular microenvironments ME1 and ME5

evidence_quotes.quote: Gene ontology analysis showed that the four macrophage subsets (LYVE1 + , MHCII + , TML and iron-recycling) expressed gene programs that drive angiogenesis (Supplementary Tables 23-26).
evidence_quotes.context: Section: Macrophages support prenatal skin angiogenesis

evidence_quotes.quote: Gene module expression profiles suggested that sprouting angiogenesis (growth of new vessels) was promoted by LYVE1 + and TML macrophages, blood vessel morphogenesis by LYVE1 + macrophages and endothelial cell chemotaxis by iron-recycling macrophages (Extended Data Fig. 8i and Supplementary Table 27).
evidence_quotes.context: Section: Macrophages support prenatal skin angiogenesis

evidence_quotes.quote: Predicted ligandreceptor interactions were consistent with reciprocal communication between macrophages and endothelial cells to support angiogenesis, chemotaxis and cell migration (for example, CXCL8-ACKR1 and CCL8-ACKR1)
evidence_quotes.context: Section: Macrophages support prenatal skin angiogenesis

evidence_quotes.quote: Gene set enrichment analysis results of over-expressed genes (wilcoxon, two-sided, Benjamini-Hochberg adjusted) in macrophage subsets (TML, Iron-recycling, LYVE1 + and MHCII + , macrophages).
